pr_description: This PR is an attempt to fix #101 and improve the monorepo support of qnm. After discussing this with @mikestopcontinues and with @ronami we figured out that the monorepo support for qnm is not good enough and we tried to think on ways to improve it. These are the changes introduced in the PR: 1. in case of a monorepo, qnm used to print all of the packages with their dependencies. It was duplicated, since the monorepo packages are linked to the root `node_modules` which made them appear twice. 2. There wasn't any sign that the modules are symlinks and that they are part of the monorepo. The same command after fixing the issues above. The bold packages on the bottom was removed in favor of a symlink marks that explains that those are part of the monorepo. 3. When running the command from within a package in a monorepo, qnm treated it as a workspace of its own. The full discussion is in issue #101. This was solved by trying to find the monorepo root and run qnm on it, showing that we're within a package when applicable. When there are no modules at all. When there are modules within the package.

This PR is an attempt to fix #101 and improve the monorepo support of qnm. After discussing this with @mikestopcontinues and with @ronami we figured out that the monorepo support for qnm is not good enough and we tried to think on ways to improve it. These are the changes introduced in the PR: 1. in case of a monorepo, qnm used to print all of the packages with their dependencies. It was duplicated, since the monorepo packages are linked to the root `node_modules` which made them appear twice. 2. There wasn't any sign that the modules are symlinks and that they are part of the monorepo. The same command after fixing the issues above. The bold packages on the bottom was removed in favor of a symlink marks that explains that those are part of the monorepo. 3. When running the command from within a package in a monorepo, qnm treated it as a workspace of its own. The full discussion is in issue #101. This was solved by trying to find the monorepo root and run qnm on it, showing that we're within a package when applicable. When there are no modules at all. When there are modules within the package.

Fixes #101 

This also improves error handling for cases remote registry is not approachable or a module doesn't exist on the remote registry.
commit_message: support running within an empty package in a monorepo

Fixes #2 

Yarn doesn't add the `requiredBy` information to the `package.json` of each package, but instead emits a `yarn.lock` file which contains this information.
We parse the lock file and adds the `requiredBy` information for easy access.

Hello,

This PR:

- improves `qnm` performance with yarn lock. There was no caching for yarn lock contents and therefore it was loaded for every dependency in the output.
- Now every command shows `--why` info by default. The `--why` option was completely removed (closes #46)

Can you please also add `hacktoberfest-accepted` label for this PR, so that it counts for hacktoberfest challenge (I still hope to get all PRs done :sweat_smile:)
commit_message: feat: show dependents (why info) by default for all commands (#46)

Responding to https://github.com/ranyitz/qnm/issues/36

This is my initial take on the issue, it can be improved, but first I want to make sure that we're headed in the right direction. @ranyitz, let me know if anything should be changed to meet the standards.

## Summary
Fixes #75 by adding the information about `latest` version and when a package was modified.

Since fetching the data from npm can take time (~1 second per module), it's turned on only for single modules. list/match won't get this data, unless the `--remote` flag is passed.

The questions that this feature help answering on are, whether my version is older than the latest version and by how much time.

**A few examples:**

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11733036/138537611-1960b7ea-7e45-4f1a-9756-ce401dbecd83.png)
 
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11733036/138537575-eb80a930-118a-432e-9c2a-356479a73608.png)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11733036/138537631-e9d34aa2-12a4-4bef-8264-577d83f1ee90.png)

* This PR also removes support for node 10, because this is a CLI, It's ok to do it in a minor release. I updated commander which doesn't support node 10 anymore.

Fixes #81 

It's nice to know when a certain dependency is in a specific location in the node_modules due to the fact it was bundled to its parent module using `bundledDependencies` configuration.

This PR makes it so it will add a `(bundledDependnecies)` mark to the output in the relevant place.

## Example output
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11733036/147163092-3c9a9102-1db3-45ef-b80d-aef5dace9f78.png)
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11733036/147163146-e05a9001-302a-404d-b74e-ba63a57b2d67.png)
